texto: The bells of the Gion monastery in India echo with the warning that all things are impermanent. The blossoms of the sala trees teach us through their hues that what flourishes must fade. The proud do not prevail for long but vanish like a spring night’s dream. In time the mighty, too, succumb: all are dust before the wind.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Heike: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “The bells of the Gion monastery in India echo with the warning that all things are impermanent. T…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que motivacao, buddhism, impermanence pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.
